task_completion.description: Open the Chinook database in LibreOffice Base
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Base main window is open showing the 'chinook.odb' database.

A SQL execution window showing a successful 'CREATE TABLE ArtistNameLog' command with 'ArtistId' and 'OriginalName' columns, OR the 'ArtistNameLog' table visible in the Tables pane of the main Base window.

task_completion.description: Record original details of artists starting with 'The ' into the audit table
task_completion.visual_evidence: A SQL execution window showing a successful 'INSERT INTO ArtistNameLog' command selecting artists whose names start with 'The ', OR the 'ArtistNameLog' table opened showing records like 'The Cult', 'The Doors', etc.

task_completion.description: Update the 'Artist' table to change names to the 'Name, The' format
task_completion.visual_evidence: A SQL execution window showing a successful 'UPDATE Artist' command using string manipulation (e.g., SUBSTRING and ||), OR the 'Artist' table opened showing updated names such as 'Cult, The', 'Doors, The', 'Police, The', etc.

task_completion.description: Save the database to persist changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks the 'Save' icon on the main LibreOffice Base toolbar, uses File > Save, or the Save icon becomes greyed out indicating no unsaved changes.

integrity.description: The agent used LibreOffice Base to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: All database interactions and SQL commands are executed within the LibreOffice Base GUI (e.g., Tools > SQL), not via an external terminal SQLite client.

integrity.description: The agent used a programmatic SQL query rather than manual edits
integrity.visual_evidence: The SQL UPDATE command uses string manipulation functions (like SUBSTRING) and a WHERE clause (e.g., WHERE Name LIKE 'The %') rather than hardcoding individual artist IDs or manually editing rows in the GUI.

integrity.description: Artists not starting with 'The ' are left unchanged
integrity.visual_evidence: If the 'Artist' table is viewed, artists like 'AC/DC' (ID 1) and 'Iron Maiden' (ID 90) remain exactly as they were, without any commas or 'The' appended.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target prefix to replace is 'The ' (with a trailing space). Expected transformations for verification: ID 139 'The Cult' -> 'Cult, The'; ID 140 'The Doors' -> 'Doors, The'; ID 141 'The Police' -> 'Police, The'; ID 142 'The Rolling Stones' -> 'Rolling Stones, The'; ID 143 'The Tea Party' -> 'Tea Party, The'; ID 144 'The Who' -> 'Who, The'. Collateral damage checks: ID 1 'AC/DC' and ID 90 'Iron Maiden' must NOT be changed. Note: ID 127 is 'Red Hot Chili Peppers' (no 'The ') in the original database, so it should NOT be changed.
